devanagari: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाच । एकदोपवनं राजन्जग्मुर्यदुकुमारकाः । विहर्तुं साम्बप्रद्युम्न चारुभानुगदादयः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībādarāyaṇiruvācaekadopavanaṃ rājanjagmuryadukumārakāḥvihartuṃ sāmbapradyumna cārubhānugadādayaḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
On one occasion Sāmba, Pradyumna, Cāru, Bhānu, Gada and other princes of the Yadu clan went to a park for sports and entertainment.

devanagari: क्रीडित्वा सुचिरं तत्र विचिन्वन्तः पिपासिताः । जलं निरुदके कूपे ददृशुः सत्त्वमद्भुतम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: krīḍitvā suciraṃ tatra vicinvantaḥ pipāsitāḥjalaṃ nirudake kūpe dadṛśuḥ sattvamadbhutam
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When the king (Nṛga) was thus accosted by Kṛṣṇa of infinite forms (Kṛṣṇa, the embodiment of Bliss), he bowed down to the Lord of Lakṣmī with his crown radiant like the Sun, and spoke:

devanagari: कृकलासं गिरिनिभं वीक्ष्य विस्मितमानसाः । तस्य चोद्धरणे यत्नं चक्रुस्ते कृयान्विताः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛkalāsaṃ girinibhaṃ vīkṣya vismitamānasāḥtasya coddharaṇe yatnaṃ cakruste kṛyānvitāḥ
translation: Observing that it is a lizard as big as a hill, they marvelled in their minds. Moved with pity, they tried to lift it out.

devanagari: चर्मजैस्तान्तवैः पाशैर्बद्ध्वा पतितमर्भकाः । नाशक्नुरन्समुद्धर्तुं कृष्णायाचख्युरुत्सुकाः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: carmajaistāntavaiḥ pāśairbaddhvā patitamarbhakāḥnāśaknuransamuddhartuṃ kṛṣṇāyācakhyurutsukāḥ
translation: The boys tied the animal fallen (in the well) with leather thongs and ropes of fiber but were unable to lift it up. Overcome with curiosity and surprise, they reported the matter to Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: तत्रागत्यारविन्दाक्षो भगवान्विश्वभावनः । वीक्ष्योज्जहार वामेन तं करेण स लीलया ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāgatyāravindākṣo bhagavānviśvabhāvanaḥvīkṣyojjahāra vāmena taṃ kareṇa sa līlayā
translation: The lotus-eyed glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, the creator and protector of the world; went there (to the well). Peeping at it he sportively lifted it up by his left hand.

devanagari: स उत्तमःश्लोककराभिमृष्टो विहाय सद्यः कृकलासरूपम् । सन्तप्तचामीकरचारुवर्णः स्वर्ग्यद्भुतालङ्करणाम्बरस्रक् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa uttamaḥślokakarābhimṛṣṭo vihāya sadyaḥ kṛkalāsarūpamsantaptacāmīkaracāruvarṇaḥ svargyadbhutālaṅkaraṇāmbarasrak
translation:
Being touched by the hand of the Lord of hallowingrenown, the being instantly shuffled off his body of a chameleon and was transformed into a heavenly being adorned with wonderful ornaments, garments, and garland of flowers and distinguished by a complexion (bright) like molten gold.
[Padaratnāvalī’sṣ Text adds:]
6(A). He bowed down to the lotus-feet of Lord Mukunda (Kṛṣṇa) with his head and lay prostrate before him.

devanagari: पप्रच्छ विद्वानपि तन्निदानं जनेषु विख्यापयितुं मुकुन्दः । कस्त्वं महाभाग वरेण्यरूपो देवोत्तमं त्वां गणयामि नूनम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: papraccha vidvānapi tannidānaṃ janeṣu vikhyāpayituṃ mukundaḥkastvaṃ mahābhāga vareṇyarūpo devottamaṃ tvāṃ gaṇayāmi nūnam
translation: Although Mukunda (Kṛṣṇa) was omniscient (and as such knew the reason), he enquired of him (the newly transformed god) with a view to making known to the public the cause of his birth in the species of lizards “Who are you, O glorious person blessed with most excellent personality. I believe you must be a great god.

devanagari: दशामिमां वा कतमेन कर्मणा सम्प्रापितोऽस्यतदर्हः सुभद्र । आत्मानमाख्याहि विवित्सतां नो यन्मन्यसे नः क्षममत्र वक्तुम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: daśāmimāṃ vā katamena karmaṇā samprāpito'syatadarhaḥ subhadraātmānamākhyāhi vivitsatāṃ no yanmanyase naḥ kṣamamatra vaktum
translation: My good friend! What act of yours brought you to such a wretched plight which you certainly did not deserve. We are curious to hear your story. Be pleased to narrate it to us if you deem us worthy of being told.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति स्म राजा सम्पृष्टः कृष्णेनानन्तमूर्तिना । माधवं प्रणिपत्याह किरीटेनार्कवर्चसा ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaiti sma rājā sampṛṣṭaḥ kṛṣṇenānantamūrtināmādhavaṃ praṇipatyāha kirīṭenārkavarcasā

devanagari: नृग उवाच । नृगो नाम नरेन्द्रो ऽहमिक्ष्वाकुतनयः प्रभो । दानिष्वाख्यायमानेषु यदि ते कर्णमस्पृशम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: nṛga uvācanṛgo nāma narendro 'hamikṣvākutanayaḥ prabhodāniṣvākhyāyamāneṣu yadi te karṇamaspṛśam
translation:
Nṛga submitted:
O Lord! I am a king called Nṛga, the son of Ikṣvāku. My name might have reached your ears if a list of liberal donors has been (at any time) mentioned to you.

devanagari: किं नु तेऽविदितं नाथ सर्वभूतात्मसाक्षिणः । कालेनाव्याहतदृशो वक्ष्येऽथापि तवाज्ञया ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ nu te'viditaṃ nātha sarvabhūtātmasākṣiṇaḥkālenāvyāhatadṛśo vakṣye'thāpi tavājñayā
translation: What is there that is unknown to you who abide as a witness in the hearts of all? And your vision and knowledge are unobstructed by Time. It is, however, in obedience to your command that I recount my history.

devanagari: यावत्यः सिकता भूमेर्यावत्यो दिवि तारकाः । यावत्यो वर्षधाराश्च तावतीरददं स्म गाः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvatyaḥ sikatā bhūmeryāvatyo divi tārakāḥyāvatyo varṣadhārāśca tāvatīradadaṃ sma gāḥ
translation: I donated as religious gift to Brāhmaṇas as many cows as there are sand-grains on the earth or as many as there are stars in the heavens or as many drops in the showers of rain,

devanagari: पयस्विनीस्तरुणीः शीलरूप गुणोपपन्नाः कपिला हेमसृङ्गीः । न्यायार्जिता रूप्यखुराः सवत्सा दुकूलमालाभरणा ददावहम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: payasvinīstaruṇīḥ śīlarūpa guṇopapannāḥ kapilā hemasṛṅgīḥnyāyārjitā rūpyakhurāḥ savatsā dukūlamālābharaṇā dadāvaham
translation: All those cows were youthful, milk-yielding, possessed of good nature, of beautiful form and excellent breed. They were tawny in colour, had their hoṛns plated in gold and hoofs in silver. They were gifted along with their calves all duly covered with silk garment, garlands and ornaments. And everyone of them was legitimately obtained by me (by paying their price in well-earned money).

devanagari: स्वलङ्कृतेभ्यो गुणशीलवद्भ्यः सीदत्कुटुम्बेभ्य ऋतव्रतेभ्यः । तपःश्रुतब्रह्मवदान्यसद्भ्यः प्रादां युवभ्यो द्विजपुङ्गवेभ्यः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: svalaṅkṛtebhyo guṇaśīlavadbhyaḥ sīdatkuṭumbebhya ṛtavratebhyaḥtapaḥśrutabrahmavadānyasadbhyaḥ prādāṃ yuvabhyo dvijapuṅgavebhyaḥ
translation: I used to give them to youthful prominent Brāhmaṇas possessing excellent qualities and noble character; who, though coming from poverty-stricken families, were noted for their vow of truthfulness and were endowed with penance, learning or knowledge of the Vedas, spiritual lore and generosity.

devanagari: गोभूहिरण्यायतनाश्वहस्तिनः कन्याः सदासीस्तिलरूप्यशय्याः । वासांसि रत्नानि परिच्छदान्रथानिष्टं च यज्ञैश्चरितं च पूर्तम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: gobhūhiraṇyāyatanāśvahastinaḥ kanyāḥ sadāsīstilarūpyaśayyāḥvāsāṃsi ratnāni paricchadānrathāniṣṭaṃ ca yajñaiścaritaṃ ca pūrtam
translation: I gave as religious gifts cows, lands, gold, houses, horses, elephants, girls (in marriage) along with maid-servants, a lot of sesamum seeds, silver, beds, garments, precious stones, utensils and furniture (necessary) for (unfurnished) houses as well as chariots. I performed a number of sacrifices and constructed wells, tanks and other works of social welfare.

devanagari: कस्यचिद्द्विजमुख्यस्य भ्रष्टा गौर्मम गोधने । सम्पृक्ताविदुषा सा च मया दत्ता द्विजातये ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: kasyaciddvijamukhyasya bhraṣṭā gaurmama godhanesampṛktāviduṣā sā ca mayā dattā dvijātaye
translation: (On one occasion) a cow belonging to a prominent Brāhmaṇa strayed and mingled in the herd of my cattle. Not. being aware of this, it was gifted by me to another Brāhmaṇa.

devanagari: तां नीयमानां तत्स्वामी दृष्ट्रोवाच ममेति तम् । ममेति परिग्राह्याह नृगो मे दत्तवानिति ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ nīyamānāṃ tatsvāmī dṛṣṭrovāca mameti tammameti parigrāhyāha nṛgo me dattavāniti
translation: As the cow was being taken away, the original owner of the cow saw it and asserted to him, “This cow belongs to me”. The recipient of the cow as a gift argued, “The cow is mine, for king Nṛga has given it to me as a religious gift”.

devanagari: विप्रौ विवदमानौ मामूचतुः स्वार्थसाधकौ । भवान्दातापहर्तेति तच्छ्रुत्वा मेऽभवद्भ्रमः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: viprau vivadamānau māmūcatuḥ svārthasādhakaubhavāndātāpaharteti tacchrutvā me'bhavadbhramaḥ
translation: Both the disputant Brāhmaṇas, each trying to establish his claim, (approached me and) said, “You are the donor of the cow to me”. The other asserted, “In that case, you have lifted my cow.” And I became confused and embarrassed to hear their claims.

devanagari: अनुनीतावुभौ विप्रौ धर्मकृच्छ्रगतेन वै । गवां लक्षं प्रकृष्टानां दास्याम्येषा प्रदीयताम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: anunītāvubhau viprau dharmakṛcchragatena vaigavāṃ lakṣaṃ prakṛṣṭānāṃ dāsyāmyeṣā pradīyatām
translation: Caught in such a religious predicament, I tried to conciliate them with a humble entreaty. “I shall give a hundred thousand excellent cows (all better than this). Please give in exchange this cow.

devanagari: भवन्तावनुगृह्णीतां किङ्करस्याविजानतः । समुद्धरतं मां कृच्छ्रात्पतन्तं निरयेऽशुचौ ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: bhavantāvanugṛhṇītāṃ kiṅkarasyāvijānataḥsamuddharataṃ māṃ kṛcchrātpatantaṃ niraye'śucau
translation: Both of you be pleased to condone this fault which has been unknowingly committed by me—your humble servant. Be pleased to lift me up from this difficult situation whereby I shall be falling in a dirty hell.

devanagari: नाहं प्रतीच्छे वै राजन्नित्युक्त्वा स्वाम्यपाक्रमत् । नान्यद्गवामप्ययुतमिच्छामीत्यपरो ययौ ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: nāhaṃ pratīcche vai rājannityuktvā svāmyapākramatnānyadgavāmapyayutamicchāmītyaparo yayau
translation:
The original owner of the cow declared, “I am not willing to accept (your offer at all) and he left. The other (new recepient owner) went away saying, “I do not wish to have ten thousand cows in exchange for this cow.”
21 A. O Supreme Lord! May be due to your Māyā power or your will expressed through force of time, I died. O Lord! I narrate in your presence where I was taken away by the force of my destiny.

devanagari: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे यामैर्दूतैर्नीतो यमक्षयम् । यमेन पृष्टस्तत्राहं देवदेव जगत्पते ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: etasminnantare yāmairdūtairnīto yamakṣayamyamena pṛṣṭastatrāhaṃ devadeva jagatpate
translation:
Bhāgavata Candrikā’s Text adds:
O God of gods! In the meanwhile (after death) I was carried away by the messenger of Yama to Yama’s abode. O Lord of the world! I was asked there by the god of death:

devanagari: पूर्वं त्वमशुभं भुङ्क्ष उताहो नृपते शुभम् । नान्तं दानस्य धर्मस्य पश्ये लोकस्य भास्वतः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: pūrvaṃ tvamaśubhaṃ bhuṅkṣa utāho nṛpate śubhamnāntaṃ dānasya dharmasya paśye lokasya bhāsvataḥ
translation: “Would you like to reap the fruit of your evil acts first or would you like to enjoy the fruits of your meritorious acts first. I do not see the limit of the charitable and religious acts performed by you nor of the glorious world that you have attained thereby.”

devanagari: पूर्वं देवाशुभं भुञ्ज इति प्राह पतेति सः । तावदद्रा क्षमात्मानं कृकलासं पतन्प्रभो ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: pūrvaṃ devāśubhaṃ bhuñja iti prāha pateti saḥtāvadadrā kṣamātmānaṃ kṛkalāsaṃ patanprabho
translation: I said, “O god! I shall first suffer for my inauspicious acts”. He commanded, “Fall down” and immediately I fell and while falling, I found myself transformed into a lizard, O Almighty Lord.

devanagari: ब्रह्मण्यस्य वदान्यस्य तव दासस्य केशव । स्मृतिर्नाद्यापि विध्वस्ता भवत्सन्दर्शनार्थिनः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmaṇyasya vadānyasya tava dāsasya keśavasmṛtirnādyāpi vidhvastā bhavatsandarśanārthinaḥ
translation: O Keśava! I am your servant (devotee) who have been friendly and charitable to Brāhmaṇas. I have been earnestly craving and striving to have your direct revelation. It is hence that my memory is still unblurred.

devanagari: स त्वं कथं मम विभोऽपिथः परात्मा । योगेश्वरः श्रुतिदृशामलहृद्विभाव्यः । साक्षादधोक्षज उरुव्यसनान्धबुद्धेः । स्यान्मेऽनुदृश्य इह यस्य भव...
english_devnagari: sa tvaṃ kathaṃ mama vibho'pithaḥ parātmāyogeśvaraḥ śrutidṛśāmalahṛdvibhāvyaḥsākṣādadhokṣaja uruvyasanāndhabuddheḥsyānme'nudṛśya iha yasya bhavāpavargaḥ
translation: O Omnipresent Lord! You are beyond the ken of sense perception. You are the Supreme Soul to be contemplated and visualized in their pure hearts with the help of the ‘eye’ (knowledge) of Upaniṣads by masters of yoga. (Such as you are) I am amazed as to how you came directly within the range of my sight—I whose reason is blinded by extreme miseries—for, you are visualized by those who are to be emancipated from saṃsāra (Hence I believe I shall be released and this is my last birth).

devanagari: देवदेव जगन्नाथ गोविन्द पुरुषोत्तम । नारायण हृषीकेश पुण्यश्लोकाच्युताव्यय ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: devadeva jagannātha govinda puruṣottamanārāyaṇa hṛṣīkeśa puṇyaślokācyutāvyaya
translation: O God of gods! Lord of the world! O Govinda (protector of cows or master of senses)? O Supreme Man! O Nārāyaṇa! O Hṛṣīkeśa (the Master of Sense-organs; their prime mover)! O Lord of hallowing renown! O Infallible Lord! O Immutable God! O Kṛṣṇa! Give n:e leave to return to my celestial state. O Lord! (I pray) wherever I may be (i.e. in whatever species I be born) my mind have asylum at your feet.

devanagari: अनुजानीहि मां कृष्ण यान्तं देवगतिं प्रभो । यत्र क्वापि सतश्चेतो भूयान्मे त्वत्पदास्पदम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: anujānīhi māṃ kṛṣṇa yāntaṃ devagatiṃ prabhoyatra kvāpi sataśceto bhūyānme tvatpadāspadam
translation: O God of gods! Lord of the world! O Govinda (protector of cows or master of senses)? O Supreme Man! O Nārāyaṇa! O Hṛṣīkeśa (the Master of Sense-organs; their prime mover)! O Lord of hallowing renown! O Infallible Lord! O Immutable God! O Kṛṣṇa! Give n:e leave to return to my celestial state. O Lord! (I pray) wherever I may be (i.e. in whatever species I be born) my mind have asylum at your feet.

devanagari: नमस्ते सर्वभावाय ब्रह्मणेऽनन्तशक्तये । कृष्णाय वासुदेवाय योगानां पतये नमः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: namaste sarvabhāvāya brahmaṇe'nantaśaktayekṛṣṇāya vāsudevāya yogānāṃ pataye namaḥ
translation: Hail to you the source (creator) of everything! The Master of Māyā potency! The final resort (or abode) of all beings! O Kṛṣṇa,[1] the Lord of all yogas, I bow down to you. (Though I am unwilling to part from you of above description, I take my leave of you to enjoy the fruits of actions conferred upon me by you”.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes a Smṛti (Purāṇic?) source which interpretes Kṛṣṇa as Brahman.
kṛṣir bhū-vācakaḥ śabdaḥ ṇaśca nirvṛti-vācakaḥ /
tayor aikyam param brahma kṛṣṇa ityabhidhīyate //

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वा तं परिक्रम्य पादौ स्पृष्ट्वा स्वमौलिना । अनुज्ञातो विमानाग्र्यमारुहत्पश्यतां नृणाम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvā taṃ parikramya pādau spṛṣṭvā svamaulināanujñāto vimānāgryamāruhatpaśyatāṃ nṛṇām
translation: Submitting to him this prayer, Nṛga circumambulated him and touched the feet of the Lord with his diadem. With his permission, he ascended an excellent aerial car, even as all people were looking on.

devanagari: कृष्णः परिजनं प्राह भगवान्देवकीसुतः । ब्रह्मण्यदेवो धर्मात्मा राजन्याननुशिक्षयन् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇaḥ parijanaṃ prāha bhagavāndevakīsutaḥbrahmaṇyadevo dharmātmā rājanyānanuśikṣayan
translation: Lord Kṛṣṇa! The son of Devakī, the God who is friendly to Brāhmaṇas—nay the very Dharma incarnate—spoke to person around him with a view to instruct those born in Kṣattriya class.

devanagari: दुर्जरं बत ब्रह्मस्वं भुक्तमग्नेर्मनागपि । तेजीयसोऽपि किमुत राज्ञां ईश्वरमानिनाम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: durjaraṃ bata brahmasvaṃ bhuktamagnermanāgapitejīyaso'pi kimuta rājñāṃ īśvaramāninām
translation: “The property of Brāhmaṇas, howsoever little it may be, if misappropriated, is indigestible even to the Fire-god (i.e. a person of immeasurable power and glory like the Fire god); what need be said that it is certainly so in the case of kings who, out of (empty) pride, regard themselves as the rulers (of the world).

devanagari: नाहं हालाहलं मन्ये विषं यस्य प्रतिक्रिया । ब्रह्मस्वं हि विषं प्रोक्तं नास्य प्रतिविधिर्भुवि ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: nāhaṃ hālāhalaṃ manye viṣaṃ yasya pratikriyābrahmasvaṃ hi viṣaṃ proktaṃ nāsya pratividhirbhuvi
translation: I do not consider the most deadly poison Halāhala as the real fatal poison, as, there is an antidote against it. But the property of a Brāhmaṇa (if misappropriated) is the real poison, as it cannot be counteracted by any remedy in the world.

devanagari: हिनस्ति विषमत्तारं वह्निरद्भिः प्रशाम्यति । कुलं समूलं दहति ब्रह्मस्वारणिपावकः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: hinasti viṣamattāraṃ vahniradbhiḥ praśāmyatikulaṃ samūlaṃ dahati brahmasvāraṇipāvakaḥ
translation: Poison kills (only one person viz.) the swallower. The fire is extinguished with water. But the fire ignited out of churning the Araṇis in the form of (misappropxiated) Brāhmaṇa property burns the whole race along with its roots.

devanagari: ब्रह्मस्वं दुरनुज्ञातं भुक्तं हन्ति त्रिपूरुषम् । प्रसह्य तु बलाद्भुक्तं दश पूर्वान्दशापरान् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmasvaṃ duranujñātaṃ bhuktaṃ hanti tripūruṣamprasahya tu balādbhuktaṃ daśa pūrvāndaśāparān
translation: A Brāhmaṇa’s property used without his (willing) permission (or with permission under duress) ruins three generations (the enjoyer, his son and grandson). But if usurped and enjoyed per force, it spells disaster to ten generations of ancestors (by forcing them to hell) and ten generation of successors after him.

devanagari: राजानो राजलक्ष्म्यान्धा नात्मपातं विचक्षते । निरयं येऽभिमन्यन्ते ब्रह्मस्वं साधु बालिशाः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: rājāno rājalakṣmyāndhā nātmapātaṃ vicakṣatenirayaṃ ye'bhimanyante brahmasvaṃ sādhu bāliśāḥ
translation: [2] Kings blinded with the pride of their sovereign power and affluence, are incapable of seeing their own fall. Those kings who regard it good to covet Brāhmaṇa’s property which is a veritable hell, are fools.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā construes differently: Those kings who regard it good to covet the property of a Brāhmaṇa are blinded by sovereign power and wealth, They do not foresee and think over their falling into the hell. They are extremely stupid.

devanagari: गृह्णन्ति यावतः पांशून्क्रन्दतामश्रुबिन्दवः । विप्राणां हृतवृत्तीनाम्वदान्यानां कुटुम्बिनाम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhṇanti yāvataḥ pāṃśūnkrandatāmaśrubindavaḥviprāṇāṃ hṛtavṛttīnāmvadānyānāṃ kuṭumbinām
translation: Those uncontrolled dictatorial monarchs, their descendants and relatives who deprive Brāhmaṇas of bountiful nature but with large family responsibilities, of their means of livelihood and their properry are subjected to torture (lit. are cooked) in the hell called Kumbhīpāka[3] for as many years as the number of particles of earth moistened by the tears shed by those Brāhmaṇas while crying.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
One of the 28 hells in which the wicked are baked like potter’s earthen-ware or are immersed in boiling oil.

devanagari: राजानो राजकुल्याश्च तावतोऽब्दान्निरङ्कुशाः । कुम्भीपाकेषु पच्यन्ते ब्रह्मदायापहारिणः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: rājāno rājakulyāśca tāvato'bdānniraṅkuśāḥkumbhīpākeṣu pacyante brahmadāyāpahāriṇaḥ
translation: Those uncontrolled dictatorial monarchs, their descendants and relatives who deprive Brāhmaṇas of bountiful nature but with large family responsibilities, of their means of livelihood and their properry are subjected to torture (lit. are cooked) in the hell called Kumbhīpāka[3] for as many years as the number of particles of earth moistened by the tears shed by those Brāhmaṇas while crying.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
One of the 28 hells in which the wicked are baked like potter’s earthen-ware or are immersed in boiling oil.

devanagari: स्वदत्तां परदत्तां वा ब्रह्मवृत्तिं हरेच्च यः । षष्टिवर्षसहस्राणि विष्ठायां जायते कृमिः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: svadattāṃ paradattāṃ vā brahmavṛttiṃ harecca yaḥṣaṣṭivarṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭhāyāṃ jāyate kṛmiḥ
translation: He who confiscates the means of livelihood of a Brāhmaṇa irrespective of its being donated to him by himself or by another person, becomes a worm in the faeces for sixty thousand years.

devanagari: न मे ब्रह्मधनं भूयाद्यद्गृध्वाल्पायुषो नराः । पराजिताश्च्युता राज्याद्भवन्त्युद्वेजिनोऽहयः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: na me brahmadhanaṃ bhūyādyadgṛdhvālpāyuṣo narāḥparājitāścyutā rājyādbhavantyudvejino'hayaḥ
translation: (I strongly wish that) I may not come in possession of the wealth or property of a Brāhmaṇa (at any time and in any. way), by coveting which people are cut short in their prime, kings become defeated and are deprived of their kingdoms and (after death) are born as formidable serpents.

devanagari: विप्रं कृतागसमपि नैव द्रुह्यत मामकाः । घ्नन्तं बहु शपन्तं वा नमस्कुरुत नित्यशः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: vipraṃ kṛtāgasamapi naiva druhyata māmakāḥghnantaṃ bahu śapantaṃ vā namaskuruta nityaśaḥ
translation: O my kinsmen! Abstain from hatred or injury (in retaliation) to a Brāhmaṇa who has offended you, attacked you or reproached you (with words of abuse). Always pay them your respects.

devanagari: यथाहं प्रणमे विप्राननुकालं समाहितः । तथा नमत यूयं च योऽन्यथा मे स दण्डभाक् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: yathāhaṃ praṇame viprānanukālaṃ samāhitaḥtathā namata yūyaṃ ca yo'nyathā me sa daṇḍabhāk
translation: Just as I bow to Brāhmaṇas regularly with a calm and collected mind, similarly you also salute to them. He who fails to do so, shall be recepient of the punishment at my hands.

devanagari: ब्राह्मणार्थो ह्यपहृतो हर्तारं पातयत्यधः । अजानन्तमपि ह्येनं नृगं ब्राह्मणगौरिव ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: brāhmaṇārtho hyapahṛto hartāraṃ pātayatyadhaḥajānantamapi hyenaṃ nṛgaṃ brāhmaṇagauriva
translation: The property of Brāhmaṇa, even though misappropriated without being aware of it, brings about the downfall of the sinner just as the cow of the Brāhmaṇa brought about the fall of king Nṛga though he was ignorant of misappropriation.”

devanagari: एवं विश्राव्य भगवान्मुकुन्दो द्वारकौकसः । पावनः सर्वलोकानां विवेश निजमन्दिरम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ viśrāvya bhagavānmukundo dvārakaukasaḥpāvanaḥ sarvalokānāṃ viveśa nijamandiram
translation:
3 As you have listened to this. O chastiser of enemies, you should always (try to) adore the saintly people. If you desire to have the final emancipation, the property of a Brāhmaṇa should never be misappropriated by you.
[back to top]

devanagari: अथ चतुःषष्टितमोऽध्यायः 10. ॥ 64 ॥
english_devnagari: atha catuḥṣaṣṭitamo'dhyāyaḥ 10 .
